lines.en_lit: The school of Rabbi Yishmael taught a fortiori inference: Just as golden garments, with which Priest does not enter the innermost sanctum, requires immersion, white garments, with which Priest enters the innermost sanctum, is it not right that require immersion? can be refuted: What to golden garments, as atonement they is extensive? Rather, it is derived Rabbi

Rabbi said: From where five immersions and ten sanctifications High Priest immerses and sanctifies on the day the verse states: “He shall be dressed in a sacred linen tunic this you learned: Anyone who moves from service, to service, requires immersion. We found from golden garments to white garments; from where from white garments to golden garments?

The school of Rabbi Yishmael taught a fortiori inference: Just as white garments, which atonement they is minimal immersion is required, golden garments, which atonement they is extensive is it not right that immersion should be required?

can be refuted: What to white garments, as he enters with them to the innermost sanctum? is that which is taught states: “They are sacred garments, and he shall wash his flesh in water and then put them on”

And they are five services: daily morning offering, in golden garments; the service of the day, in white garments; his ram ram of the people, in golden garments. spoon and the coal pan in white garments. daily afternoon offering in golden garments

And from where that each and every immersion requires two sanctifications the verse states: and he shall remove And he shall wash “And he shall wash and put on That is written to immersion. If it is not a matter to immersion, which is derived from “They are sacred garments” render it a matter to sanctification.
And let the Merciful One write in language of sanctification This teach us that immersion is like sanctification. Just as sanctification in a sacred area, so too, immersion in a sacred area,
And Rabbi Yehuda, from where does he sanctification He derives it from Rabbi Elazar, son of Rabbi Shimon:

Rav Ḥisda said: That Rabbi is to the exclusion of Rabbi Meir and to the exclusion of the Rabbis. It is to the exclusion of the Rabbis, as the Rabbis said he sanctifies he is dressed, and said: He sanctifies when he undresses. And it is to the exclusion of Rabbi Meir, as Rabbi Meir said: this last sanctification he sanctifies when he is dressed. And said: He sanctifies when he undresses.

Rav Aḥa bar Ya’akov said: Everyone agrees with regard to the second sanctification that one dresses, and afterward sanctifies What the reason as the verse states: “Or when they approach the altar lacking only approach exclusion of that who is lacking donning approach
Rav Aḥa, son Rava, said to Rav Ashi: Neither does Rav Ḥisda, agree with Rav Aḥa, nor does Rav Aḥa, agree with Rav Ḥisda, As, if do to Rabbi there are fifteen sanctifications.

They brought him the the daily offering, which he slaughtered [keratzo]. What keratzo? Ulla said: It is a term killing. Rav Naḥman bar Yitzḥak said: What is the verse “Egypt is a very fair heifer, but the keretz out of north is come, it is come” From where inferred as translated by Rav Yosef: Egypt is a fair kingdom, but murderous nations will come upon it from the north. slaughtered to what extent Ulla said: with the majority of two

And similarly, Rav Yoḥanan said: With the majority of two And Reish Lakish also held: With the majority of two as Reish Lakish said: Once we learned the majority of one like the whole, why was we learn the majority of one in a bird and the majority two in an animal? since we learned: They brought him the the daily offering, which he slaughtered and a different completed slaughter on his behalf received the blood and sprinkled it might did not complete would be invalid.
might did not complete would be invalid? If so, is service another And it was taught All Yom Kippur services are valid only by this is what is saying: might it would be invalid by rabbinic law;
